headline: SILVER ROCKETS 5% TO $79 — BEST DAY SINCE MID-MARCH AS IRAN DEAL HAMMERS DOLLAR
snippet: Silver surged 4.85% to $79/oz on April 14 — its strongest single-day move since mid-March — after progress in US-Iran ceasefire talks weakened the dollar and pushed oil below $100 a barrel, easing inflation fears. The metal is up 144% year-over-year, and a sixth consecutive annual supply deficit is adding structural fuel beneath the geopolitical catalyst.

headline: RARE EARTH MARKET ENTERS HYPERGROWTH PHASE — DEMAND SURGE HITS UNDERDEVELOPED WESTERN SUPPLY
snippet: A new market assessment published April 14 finds rare earth demand entering a structural acceleration phase, driven by defense spending, EV motors, and wind turbine buildout — while Western supply chains remain critically underdeveloped. The report follows China Northern Rare Earth's 44% Q2 price hike and escalating U.S.-China tensions over critical minerals access.

headline: GOLD'S HISTORIC FLIGHT: April 2026 Surge Signals Growing Anxiety Over Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: With gold near $4,800/oz and up 44% year-over-year, this April 13 analysis argues the metal is no longer pricing short-term geopolitics — it's pricing the long-term erosion of fiscal credibility across the G7 as debt ceilings and central bank credibility fray. The piece makes the case that retail investors chasing gold miners are actually late to a macro repricing that started years ago.

headline: ABRASILVER DRILLS 171 METRES AT 0.83 g/t GOLD IN ARGENTINA — OCULTO EAST EXPANDS BEYOND PIT LIMITS
snippet: AbraSilver Resource (TSX-V: ABRA) reported April 13 that Phase VI drilling at its Diablillos project in Argentina returned 171 metres at 0.83 g/t gold (including 9m at 3.46 g/t), extending oxide gold-silver mineralization several hundred metres beyond the current open-pit boundary. The company is targeting a Definitive Feasibility Study release in Q2 2026 as the resource footprint continues to grow.

headline: COPPER CLEARS $13,000/TONNE — 30% YEAR-OVER-YEAR GAIN FUELED BY TARIFF FEARS AND SUPPLY ANXIETY
snippet: Copper broke through $13,000/tonne on April 13 — up more than 30% year-over-year — driven by tariff-linked supply anxiety even as LME warehouse inventories hit an 8-year high, a sign the market is looking through near-term stockpiles at structural long-term deficits. 24/7 Wall St. identifies the best pure-play copper ETF for retail investors seeking direct price exposure.

headline: GoldSeek SWOT: China Vacuuming Up Silver — 790+ Tonnes in Early 2026 as Sixth Deficit Year Looms
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly precious metals SWOT analysis, published April 13, highlights China absorbing more than 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026 — largely for solar panel manufacturing — as the market heads into a sixth consecutive annual supply deficit. COT positioning data shows large speculators remain structurally under-committed to silver, a setup that has historically preceded sharp upside moves.

headline: G MINING BUYS G2 GOLDFIELDS FOR C$3 BILLION — DISTRICT-SCALE GUYANA GOLD HUB TARGETS 500K OZ/YEAR
snippet: GoldSeek's April 13 SWOT analysis breaks down the week's marquee M&A: G Mining Ventures (TSX: GMIN) agreed to acquire adjacent G2 Goldfields in an all-stock deal that combines the Oko West and Oko-Ghanie projects in Guyana into a district-scale gold complex targeting 500,000+ oz/year production. The analysis weighs deal accretion math against the premium paid and asks whether junior gold M&A at 2026 prices still makes sense.

headline: Money Metals: China's SULFURIC ACID BAN Could Trigger a Physical Silver Supply Crisis
snippet: Money Metals' Mike Maharrey traces a supply chain most analysts miss: China's May sulfuric acid export ban cuts copper mine output, and since roughly 70% of the world's silver is a byproduct of copper production, the already-strained silver market — running a 95 million oz annual deficit — faces an additional supply shock with no quick fix. A critical structural angle that is almost entirely absent from mainstream financial media coverage.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE SURGES 78% IN CHINA — YICHUN PERMIT CANCELLATIONS AND VAT SHIFT SHOCK MARKET
snippet: China's lithium carbonate price surged 78% in just over a month as Yichun authorities moved to cancel several lithium mining permits and China's April 1 VAT export rebate cut on Li-ion batteries triggered a wave of front-loading demand. At CNY 165,000/tonne, lithium is at its highest since late 2023 — a historic reversal for a metal that had been beaten down for two years.

headline: CHINA NORTHERN RARE EARTH SLAMS Q2 PRICES UP 44% — SEVENTH STRAIGHT QUARTERLY HIKE
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth Group set its Q2 2026 concentrate transaction price at RMB 38,804/tonne on April 10 — a 44.6% jump from Q1, representing the largest single-quarter increase since the pricing mechanism reform in 2023 and the seventh consecutive quarterly hike. Western manufacturers dependent on Chinese rare earth inputs are facing rapidly rising input costs with few alternative sourcing options.
